svg.title: From speaking at your computer to a Claude Code prompt
svg.desc: A person taps a global hotkey, speaks while taking screenshots, switches to Claude Code, then presses one key to insert the transcript and screenshots.
svg.text: RIFF • FROM VOICE TO CLAUDE CODE

g.text: You're already working at your computer.
Riff stays out of the way until you need it.
A global shortcut starts listening—no terminal needed.
The computer listens while he talks.
He speaks naturally while staying in the app he was already using.
He taps the screenshot hotkey—without stopping.
His voice keeps recording while he captures the screen.
He keeps speaking as each screenshot is captured.
Riff remembers each image at the moment he talks about it.
Then he switches to Claude Code.
The terminal is ready. His captured session is waiting in Riff.
One more hotkey stops and sends the session.
Riff transcribes locally, then hands the words and images to Claude Code.
His transcript and screenshots appear in Claude Code.
The complete context lands in Claude Code, ready to send.
Update this dashboard using the changes I described.
Simplify the chart and make the headline clearer.
